content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward methods (ICM/RND) can be reframed as a self-supervised pre-training step for world models, where the intrinsic reward signal from exploring uncertain state-space regions acts as a dynamic, agent-centric labeling function for self-supervised world model memory training that outperforms static self-supervision. This hybrid framework assigns higher priority to encoding surprising, agent-relevant experiences into episodic memory rather than evenly distributing capacity across all observed states.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
